title: Office of Small Business Programs
description: The Missile Defense Agency has fielded an initial capability to protect the U.S and our allies against limited ballistic missile attack.
text:
The Missile Defense Agency (MDA) Office of Small Business Programs (OSBP) is responsible for all aspects of the MDA Small Business and Small Disadvantaged Business Utilization Programs.
Mission:
We facilitate access to ingenuity within the small business industrial base the Missile Defense Agency relies on for missile defense.
Vision:
We strive to remain an integral player and valued advisor to the workforce developing and implementing acquisition strategies supporting MDA’s foundation for missile defense, while effectively advocating for use of small businesses in our procurements.
News:
Quarterly Newsletter
POSTPONED: NDIA-TVC Missile Defense Agency Small Business Conference
We regret to inform you that the 2026 NDIA-TVC MDA Small Business Conference, scheduled for February 10 - 11 at the Von Braun Center in Huntsville, AL, has been postponed. We sincerely apologize for any inconvenience this may cause. Updated information regarding potential new dates will be shared as soon as available. See the Event Website (external link opens in new window)for updates.
We hope your marketing endeavors with the MDA will be a mutually rewarding experience.
MDA requirements are posted on https://sam.gov/content/home(external link opens in new window). Search "Contract Opportunities." On the left side of the results page under "Federal Organizations", select the three dots to filter results. In the sub-tier box enter MISSILE DEFENSE AGENCY and hit "Select".
Occasionally, MDA uses GSA contract vehicles to fulfill our requirements. Businesses must already have a Schedule. Visit https://vsc.gsa.gov/(external link opens in new window) and click on “I want a contract” at the top of the page for more information.
We welcome your questions and requests for information. Email us today at mda.outreach@mail.mil.
Advisory:
Due to increasing geopolitical tensions, the Department of Homeland Security’s (DHS) Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) issued a “Shields Up” advisory. READ MORE
